The temperature here was comparatively warm, and the thermometer maintained a mean height of more than 34° Fahrenheit. Salt water, as is well known, does not freeze so readily as fresh, but requires several degrees of cold below freezing point before it becomes solidified, and the sea was therefore still liquid. All the icebergs and floes here had come from latitudes farther north, and, if we may so express it, lived upon the cold they had brought with them. His faith in the engineer was complete; nothing could disturb it. He believed him capable of undertaking anything and succeeding in everything. The Nautilus was motionless. Its ballast tanks full, it was sitting at a depth of 1,000 meters in a comparatively unpopulated region of the ocean where only larger fish put in occasional appearances.

- Mrs. Weldon — the owner's wife, travelling with her son Jack and old Nan

In a desperate struggle the narrator "violently repulsed the madman," who "disappeared in space" (DA-001-208, DA-001-211). The over-dilated balloon then bursts, but he clings to the netting and is carried down, saved at the last by the anchor cord catching in a crevice (DA-001-213, DA-001-216).

The firm was to be "presided over by the President of the Gun Club" (TT-004-011). Against it stand foreign rivals such as Major Donellan and Boris Karkof, "semi-military man, semi-diplomat" (TT-002-016), who suspect the American bidders.
# Ural Mountains

The searchers debate the island as a possible refuge: Paganel asks "wouldn't it be advisable to stop a day or two at the Tristan d'Acunha Isles and the Amsterdam?" (SC-027-084) Yet "after a close survey, the Amsterdam Isles were rejected in their turn." (SC-027-065)
description: 'The Nebraska river whose valley the Pacific Railroad follows across the plains in Around the World in Eighty Days.'
